name: LambdaTest Cross-Browser Testing API
what_it_does: LambdaTest API for cloud-based cross-browser and cross-device testing platform. Enables AI agents to manage test sessions, retrieve test results and screenshots, manage integrations, and control automated browser tests running on LambdaTest's grid. Supports Selenium, Playwright, Cypress, and Appium testing frameworks.
use_cases: Retrieving test execution results and screenshots from test automation agents
Querying cross-browser test failures and video recordings from QA automation agents
Managing test session creation and configuration from CI/CD pipeline agents
Analyzing flaky test patterns and browser compatibility issues from test intelligence agents
not_for: Simple unit or API testing (LambdaTest is browser/UI test focused)
best_when: A QA team using LambdaTest wants AI agents to query test results, analyze failures, and integrate cross-browser testing data into CI/CD workflows.
avoid_when: You only need basic browser testing — LambdaTest's free tier is limited. Also consider BrowserStack as an alternative.

auth.notes: LambdaTest username + access key authentication (HTTP Basic Auth). Keys available in LambdaTest profile settings.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: limited automation minutes; real browser testing available
pricing.notes: Competitive pricing with BrowserStack. Free tier for personal projects.

performance.rate_limits: LambdaTest API: rate limits based on plan concurrency

known_agent_gotchas: No public MCP server — REST API integration required
Test session management requires understanding LambdaTest's session model
Screenshot and video retrieval has expiry windows — cache results promptly
Concurrency limits depend on plan — agent parallelism must respect limits
Cross-browser testing results may include large binary data (screenshots, videos)
agent_readiness.security_notes: Test screenshots may contain sensitive application UI data. SOC2 certified. HTTP Basic Auth credentials must be secured.

security_score_components.security_notes: Test screenshots may contain sensitive UI data. SOC2. Basic Auth credentials must be secured.
